content:
# Recommendations for the next plan-agent iteration (iter-100)

**Headline.** Iter-099 ran a single substantive prover lane on `BasicOpenCech.lean`'s `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` per-summand `hG` discharge (formerly L768, now L811). **No sorry closed**, but a real structural advance committed: the iter-101 plan's S1–S3 chain (`simp only [Pi.smul_apply]`, `show … (Pi.π Z₂ j').hom _`, 4-layer `← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply`) lands cleanly at L765–L779. **STREAK ESCALATION CRITERION TRIGGERED**: this is the 3rd consecutive substantive prover lane on this sorry (iter-099/iter-100/iter-101). The plan agent for the next iteration MUST NOT prescribe a 4th raw-tactic pass — escalation is mandatory.

The post-S3 goal at L811 is the deepest verified state:
```
⊢ (ConcreteCategory.hom (((-1)^↑i • Pi.lift_thing) ≫ eqToHom ⋯ ≫ Pi.π Z₂ j')) (e₁.symm (r' • y'))
   = r' • (ConcreteCategory.hom (((-1)^↑i • Pi.lift_thing) ≫ eqToHom ⋯ ≫ Pi.π Z₂ j')) (e₁.symm y')
```

S4 (scalar `(-1)^↑i •` extraction from the morphism composition) fails for **six routes**, including two that the iter-101 plan documented as escalation escapes (E1: body-local rfl-helper; in-place variant of E2: `set Pi_lift_thing`). The discrimination-tree blocker is now confirmed structural: it persists whether the smul is at the morphism level (`(n • f) ≫ g`), at the ConcreteCategory-application level (`(n • f).hom x`), or hidden behind a `set`-binder (which doesn't fold). The body-local `have h : (n • f).hom x = n • f.hom x := by intros; rfl` typechecks but `simp only [h]` reports "no progress" — the pattern-match runs before rfl-evaluation. **The E1 escape route is dead.**

## Priority 1 (iter-100, MANDATORY) — REFACTOR LANE: introduce named-codomain Pi-product helper

**Action**: schedule a single **refactor** lane (slug recommendation: `pi-lift-thing-named-binder` or `cech-summand-via-named-T`). Goal: introduce a top-level helper that bypasses the discrimination-tree issue on `Pi.lift fun i ↦ <anonymous-i-body>` by making the per-coordinate morphism family a **named binder** with explicit named codomain.

**Directive sketch** (extracted verbatim from iter-099 prover task_result § "Recommended iter-102 escalation: option E2"):

```lean
-- Top-level helper for the next iteration
theorem cechCofaceMap_pi_smul_summand_via_named
    {k : Type u} [Field k] {R : Type*} [Ring R]
    {ι₁ : Type u} (Z₁ : ι₁ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
    {ι₂ : Type u} (Z₂ : ι₂ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
    [Module R (∀ i, Z₁ i)] [Module R (∀ j, Z₂ j)]
    (h : ι₂ → ι₁)
    (T : ∀ j, Z₁ (h j) ⟶ Z₂ j)
    (e₁ : (∏ᶜ Z₁) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ i, Z₁ i)
    (e₂ : (∏ᶜ Z₂) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ j, Z₂ j)
    (hT : ∀ j (r : R) (y_j : Z₁ (h j)),
      e₂ ((T j).hom (r • y_j)) = r • e₂ ((T j).hom y_j))
    (n : ℤ) (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i) :
    -- Per-coord R-linearity of `n • (Pi.lift via T) ≫ Pi.π _ j`
    sorry
```

The point: the family `T : ∀ j, Z₁ (h j) ⟶ Z₂ j` has a **named Pi-codomain at each coordinate**, eliminating the anonymous-closure issue. The new helper is applied at the `?hG` discharge site via `refine` against `T := fun j ↦ (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (Pi.lift _).op` with `h j := j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom` peeled into a binder.

**Risk note**: the refactor lane needs to (i) verify universe hygiene (`ι₂` matches `Fin (n+1) → ↥s₀`'s universe), (ii) verify the per-coordinate hypothesis `hT` has the right shape for `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` (iter-087 L425) to discharge it, and (iii) check that the literal call site can `refine` into the named-T form with Lean's elaboration filling `T` and `h` without backtracking. The split-slot pattern (iter-098/099) is precedent for this approach.

**Estimated effort**: 1 refactor lane (60–120 lines including signature + sketch body + call-site application).

**Acceptable outcome**: file compiles after refactor with the L811 sorry replaced by `refine` against the new named-T helper, leaving a (smaller) `?hT` sub-goal for the next prover. Sorry count may go up by 1 (new top-level helper introduces a sorry) before going down by 2 (the named-T body + the L811 closure both yield).

## Priority 2 (iter-100, IF Priority 1 stalls or runs long) — alternative E3: `LinearMap.ext` route

If the refactor lane fails to compile (e.g. universe hygiene blockers like iter-098 needed `ι_int : Type u`), the next fallback is the iter-099 task_result's E3 alternative:

```lean
-- inside the L811 hG discharge
apply LinearMap.ext_iff.mpr
intro z
-- goal: LHS z = RHS z, where both sides have explicit `Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ...` applied to z
-- now `simp only [Pi.lift_apply]` or `Pi.lift_π` at value level may unfold
```

This requires a `LinearMap`-level reframing (current shape is at function-application level via `ConcreteCategory.hom`). May be lighter than E2 if it works, but does not have precedent in our project.

**Estimated effort**: 1 prover lane (40–80 lines).

## Priority 3 — DO NOT retry these dead-end classes

Add to the existing iter-099/iter-100 dead-end registry:

| Dead-end | Confirmed iter | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]; show (Pi.π Z₂ j').hom _ = r' • (Pi.π Z₂ j').hom _; ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply (×4)` | iter-099 **PARTIAL SUCCESS** (S1–S3 committed; S4–S6 dead) | **PRESERVE the partial state**; do NOT re-run S1–S3. |
| `rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]` / `simp only [...]` / `simp_rw [...]` on **post-S3** goal | iter-099 | Same discrimination-tree blocker as pre-funext — composition's LHS `(-1)^↑i • Pi.lift _` keys the same anonymous closure. |
| **Body-local `have h_smul_hom : ∀ {M N} (n : ℤ) (f : M ⟶ N) (x), (n • f).hom x = n • f.hom x := by intros; rfl` then `simp only [h_smul_hom]` / `rw [h_smul_hom]`** | **iter-099 (NEW dead-end class)** | **Pattern-match runs BEFORE rfl-evaluation; helper typechecks but fails to fire. The iter-100/iter-101 plan's E1 escape route is DEAD.** |
| `set Pi_lift_thing : (∏ᶜ Z₁) ⟶ _ := Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ...` (in-place E2 variant) | iter-099 | `set` substitutes by syntactic match; `_`-codomain ascription doesn't fold Pi.lift's anonymous-closure codomain. Top-level refactor required instead. |
| `rw [ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply Z₂ j']` (forward direction, post-funext) | iter-099 **(NEW class)** | LinearEquiv coercion `e₂ = (piIsoPi Z₂).toLinearEquiv` masks the discrimination key; use `show` def-eq pivot instead. |
| `simp only [eqToHom_naturality, Pi.lift_π_apply]` (iter-101 plan S4 step naming) | iter-099 (not tried — superseded by structural blocker on S4) | The lemma names are speculative; verify before transcribing if Priority 2 is taken. |

## Priority 4 — preserve byte-for-byte across iter-100

1. `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` body at L478–L494 (CLOSED iter-097/session 95).
2. `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` (iter-098 lemma at L513–L531 signature + L532–L537 body CLOSED iter-099/session 96).
3. `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` prefix L539–L699 — preserve.
4. Iter-099-session-97 bridge tactics at L700–L712: `rw [← Pi.smul_apply (i := j)]; refine congrFun (alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp Z₁ _ Z₂ Finset.univ _ _ e₁ e₂ ?_ r y) j`.
5. Iter-100-session-98 partial chain at L726–L747: `intro i _ r' y'; simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply]; funext j'`.
6. **NEW**: Iter-101-session-99 partial chain at L765–L779: `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]; show ... (Pi.π Z₂ j').hom _; rw [show ModuleCat.Hom.hom = ConcreteCategory.hom ..., ..., ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply (×4)]`. Comments and S4–S6 escalation analysis at L780–L810.

## Priority 5 — reusable patterns to remember

1. **`funext j'` per-coordinate pivot for Pi.lift-anonymous-closure discrimination-tree failures** *(iter-100, ESTABLISHED)*. Preserved, but **NOTE**: per-coordinate frame does NOT dissolve the discrimination-tree class when the morphism composition retains the anonymous-closure Pi.lift in its source. Use it for goal-shape simplification only.
2. **`show` def-eq pivot for `e₂ _ j' → (Pi.π Z j).hom _`** *(iter-099, NEW)*: when the LHS uses a LinearEquiv coercion (e.g. `e₂` constructed from `(piIsoPi Z).toLinearEquiv`), the equivalent piIsoPi-lemma does NOT fire via `rw` directly. Pivot the goal by `show` to put `Pi.π Z j` explicitly into both sides — this works by def-eq and unblocks subsequent `← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply` chains.
3. **4-layer `← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply` chain for absorbing nested ConcreteCategory.hom applications** *(iter-099, NEW)*: when a post-funext goal has shape `(Pi.π Z j).hom (eqToHom_hom (smul_thing.hom (e_inv x)))` (4 layered hom-applications), the chain `rw [show ModuleCat.Hom.hom = ConcreteCategory.hom (C := ModuleCat.{u} k) from rfl, ..., ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]` fuses into a single categorical morphism composition.
4. **`set h : T := n` as a scalar-type diagnostic** *(iter-100, ESTABLISHED, RE-CONFIRMED iter-099)*. Preserved.
5. **Refactor split-slot pattern for HOU-blocked single-slot lemmas** *(iter-098/099, ESTABLISHED, awaiting next deployment via the named-T helper)*.
6. **Plan-recipe transcription with one-call name-correction** *(meta-pattern, RE-CONFIRMED iter-099)*: `piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply` fires only in `simp_rw`/reverse-direction form in some frames; verify direction and form before transcribing.
7. **NEW: Body-local rfl-helper is INSUFFICIENT for discrimination-tree blockers** *(iter-099, anti-pattern)*: a `have h : ... := by intros; rfl` typechecks but does NOT bypass the discrimination tree at `simp only [h]` / `rw [h]` — the pattern-match runs before rfl-evaluation. **The escape route from iter-100/iter-101 plans is DEAD.** Use top-level refactor with named binders instead.

## Priority 6 — sorry budget guidance for iter-100

- **Iter-100 (refactor lane)**: TARGET sorries 15 in BasicOpenCech.lean **TEMPORARILY** (new top-level named-T helper signature with sorry body — same pattern as iter-098). HARD CAP: 7 in BasicOpenCech.lean (no regression beyond the new helper).
- **Iter-100 (prover lane, IF refactor lane includes a prover follow-up)**: TARGET 5 in BasicOpenCech.lean (close L811 via the new named-T helper application, AND close the helper body via the iter-098 split-slot precedent). Acceptable outcome: total sorry count 14 → 13 if both close, or 14 → 14 (helper sorry equal to L811 sorry) if only the application lands.

## Off-limits for iter-100 (same as iter-099)

- `Differentials.lean` — Mathlib-gap deferral; Route A/B decision on L636 still pending.
- `Modules/Monoidal.lean` — Mathlib gap.
- `Jacobian.lean`, `Picard/Functor.lean` — Phase C/E.
- `BasicOpenCech.lean` L903, L1227, L1255 (augmented-Čech).
- `BasicOpenCech.lean` L1445, L1474 (gated on L811 closing).

## Plan-agent warning — same blocker, 3rd iteration

The L728/L768/L811 sorry has now been the target of **3 consecutive substantive prover lanes** (iter-099, iter-100, iter-101). The streak escalation criterion is **TRIGGERED** by the iter-101 plan's own rules.

**Plan-agent must NOT re-issue any tactic chain against this sorry in iter-100. The mandate is a REFACTOR LANE.** If the plan agent has limited budget for a refactor and selects E3 (LinearMap.ext route) instead, that is acceptable, but a 4th prover-only iteration is FORBIDDEN.

## Note on the streak

Iter-101 broke the iter-098/iter-099 streak of 1-sorry-closed-per-iter (iter-100 also was zero, so this is now **2 consecutive zero-closure iterations**, both with structural advances). Eighth consecutive compile-verified prover iteration. The cycle **refactor → prover** is the production loop; iter-100 and iter-101 were both pure prover lanes that stalled on the same discrimination-tree issue. Iter-100 (next) MUST be a refactor lane per the streak criterion.
